1	## Bash
2	```
3	gh pr view 46 --json title,body,state,additions,deletions,changedFiles,headRefName
4	```
5	
6	## Bash
7	```
8	gh pr diff 46
9	```

11	## Result
12	```
13	{"additions":12,"body":"## Summary\n- Every PR Review run (and any action that walks the tree) was failing with `ENOENT: no such file or directory, statx '.claude/.claude'` — see run [27373832204](https://github.com/pantageepapa/my-harness/actions/runs/27373832204).\n- `.claude/.claude` is a symlink to the absolute path `/Users/jbae/Dev/my-harness/.claude`. It doesn't resolve on a runner.\n- Root cause: `start-wt`'s `create-worktree.sh` did `ln -s \"\\$REPO/.claude\" \"\\$WORKTREE/.claude\"` without `-n`. When the worktree already had a `.claude` symlink, `ln` followed it and wrote a new `.claude` link *inside* the real `.claude/` dir. PR #43 picked it up via `git add`.\n\n## Changes\n- Untrack `.claude/.claude` and gitignore it.\n- Make `create-worktree.sh` idempotent: skip if destination exists, use `ln -sn` so an existing symlink target isn't followed.\n\n## Test plan\n- [ ] Re-run PR Review on any open PR after merge — should no longer ENOENT.\n- [ ] Run `start-wt` against a worktree that already has the configs linked — should be a no-op for those entries instead of producing a recursive symlink.\n\n🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)","changedFiles":3,"deletions":4,"headRefName":"fix/recursive-claude-symlink","state":"OPEN","title":"fix: remove self-referential .claude/.claude symlink breaking CI"}
14	```
15	
16	## Result
17	```
18	diff --git a/.claude/.claude b/.claude/.claude
19	deleted file mode 120000
20	index 145ac56..0000000
21	--- a/.claude/.claude
22	+++ /dev/null
23	@@ -1 +0,0 @@
24	-/Users/jbae/Dev/my-harness/.claude
25	\ No newline at end of file
26	diff --git a/.claude/skills/start-wt/create-worktree.sh b/.claude/skills/start-wt/create-worktree.sh
27	index c6d0dab..5396a2a 100755
28	--- a/.claude/skills/start-wt/create-worktree.sh
29	+++ b/.claude/skills/start-wt/create-worktree.sh
30	@@ -15,11 +15,15 @@ log "Creating worktree (branch: $BRANCH)..."
31	 mkdir -p "$WORKTREE_PARENT"
32	 git worktree add -b "$BRANCH" "$WORKTREE_PATH" >/dev/null 2>&1
33	 
34	-# Symlink shared config dirs — edits stay in sync across worktrees
35	+# Symlink shared config dirs — edits stay in sync across worktrees.
36	+# Use `ln -sn` and skip if the target already exists, so that if $d is
37	+# already a symlink to a directory we don't follow it and write the new
38	+# link *inside* the real target (that's how a self-referential
39	+# `.claude/.claude` symlink got committed in PR #43).
40	 log "  Symlinking config dirs..."
41	 for d in .claude .cursor .instrumental .agent_os; do
42	-  if [ -d "${REPO_PATH}/$d" ]; then
43	-    ln -s "${REPO_PATH}/$d" "${WORKTREE_PATH}/$d"
44	+  if [ -d "${REPO_PATH}/$d" ] && [ ! -e "${WORKTREE_PATH}/$d" ] && [ ! -L "${WORKTREE_PATH}/$d" ]; then
45	+    ln -sn "${REPO_PATH}/$d" "${WORKTREE_PATH}/$d"
46	     log "    linked $d"
47	   fi
48	 done
49	diff --git a/.gitignore b/.gitignore
50	index 74eeb60..a42f1c7 100644
51	--- a/.gitignore
52	+++ b/.gitignore
53	@@ -14,3 +14,8 @@ tools/log-index.json
54	 
55	 # Per-user Claude Code overrides — keep out of the shared repo.
56	 .claude/settings.local.json
57	+
58	+# Self-referential symlink the worktree skill used to produce when re-run
59	+# inside an existing worktree (`.claude/.claude` -> repo's `.claude/`).
60	+# Tracking it broke CI with ENOENT on the runner; guard against re-add.
61	+.claude/.claude
62	```

70	```

74	1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
75	2	set -euo pipefail
76	3	
77	4	BRANCH="${1:?Usage: create-worktree.sh <branch-name>}"
78	5	REPO_PATH="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)}"
79	6	PROJECT_NAME="$(basename "$REPO_PATH")"
80	7	WORKTREE_PARENT="$(dirname "$REPO_PATH")/${PROJECT_NAME}-worktrees"
81	8	WORKTREE_PATH="${WORKTREE_PARENT}/${BRANCH//\//-}"
82	9	
83	10	# Progress to /dev/tty — stdout reserved for Claude
84	11	log() { echo "$*" > /dev/tty 2>/dev/null || true; }
85	12	
86	13	log "Creating worktree (branch: $BRANCH)..."
87	14	
88	15	mkdir -p "$WORKTREE_PARENT"
89	16	git worktree add -b "$BRANCH" "$WORKTREE_PATH" >/dev/null 2>&1
90	17	
91	18	# Symlink shared config dirs — edits stay in sync across worktrees
92	19	log "  Symlinking config dirs..."
93	20	for d in .claude .cursor .instrumental .agent_os; do
94	21	  if [ -d "${REPO_PATH}/$d" ]; then
95	22	    ln -s "${REPO_PATH}/$d" "${WORKTREE_PATH}/$d"
96	23	    log "    linked $d"
97	24	  fi
98	25	done
99	26	
100	27	# Copy env files — these often need per-worktree tweaks
101	28	log "  Copying env files..."
102	29	for f in .env .env.local; do
103	30	  if [ -f "${REPO_PATH}/$f" ]; then
104	31	    cp "${REPO_PATH}/$f" "${WORKTREE_PATH}/$f"
105	32	    log "    copied $f"
106	33	  fi
107	34	done
108	35	
109	36	# Copy gitignored Jira config — tracked .jira files arrive via git checkout,
110	37	# but config.yml (and its backup) are per-clone and need to travel.
111	38	for f in .jira/config.yml .jira/config.yml.bkp; do
112	39	  if [ -f "${REPO_PATH}/$f" ]; then
113	40	    mkdir -p "${WORKTREE_PATH}/$(dirname "$f")"
114	41	    cp "${REPO_PATH}/$f" "${WORKTREE_PATH}/$f"
115	42	    log "    copied $f"
116	43	  fi
117	44	done
118	45	
119	46	log "Worktree ready."
120	47	
121	48	# The only thing on stdout — Claude parses this
122	49	echo "WORKTREE_PATH=$WORKTREE_PATH"
123	50	
124	```

126	## Assistant
127	The changes are clean and correct. The fix correctly addresses the root cause:
128	
129	1. Removes the absolute-path symlink that broke CI runners
130	2. Adds the `.gitignore` guard to prevent re-addition
131	3. The idempotency condition in `create-worktree.sh` correctly handles all destination states (non-existent, valid symlink, broken symlink via `[ ! -L ]`), and `ln -sn` adds good defense-in-depth
132	
133	Nothing to flag. Posting a top-level LGTM.
134	
135	## Bash
136	```
137	gh pr comment 46 --body "Looks good to me. The fix is minimal and correct: the idempotency guard (\`[ ! -e ] && [ ! -L ]\`) covers all three destination states (absent, valid symlink, broken symlink), and \`ln -sn\` adds sound defense-in-depth. The \`.gitignore\` entry closes the re-add path cleanly."
138	```

142	https://github.com/pantageepapa/my-harness/pull/46#issuecomment-4687837953
143	```

When the worktree already had a `.claude` symlink, `ln` followed it and wrote a new `.claude` link *inside* the real `.claude/` dir. PR #43 picked it up via `git add`.\n\n## Changes\n- Untrack `.claude/.claude` and gitignore it.\n- Make `create-worktree.sh` idempotent: skip if destination exists, use `ln -sn` so an existing symlink target isn't followed.\n\n## Test plan\n- [ ] Re-run PR Review on any open PR after merge — should no longer ENOENT.\n- [ ] Run `start-wt` against a worktree that already has the configs linked — should be a no-op for those entries instead of producing a recursive symlink.\n\n🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)","changedFiles":3,"deletions":4,"headRefName":"fix/recursive-claude-symlink","state":"OPEN","title":"fix: remove self-referential .claude/.claude symlink breaking CI"}
```

```
1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	set -euo pipefail
3	
4	BRANCH="${1:?Usage: create-worktree.sh <branch-name>}"
5	REPO_PATH="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)}"
6	PROJECT_NAME="$(basename "$REPO_PATH")"
7	WORKTREE_PARENT="$(dirname "$REPO_PATH")/${PROJECT_NAME}-worktrees"
8	WORKTREE_PATH="${WORKTREE_PARENT}/${BRANCH//\//-}"
9	
10	# Progress to /dev/tty — stdout reserved for Claude
11	log() { echo "$*" > /dev/tty 2>/dev/null || true; }
12	
13	log "Creating worktree (branch: $BRANCH)..."
14	
15	mkdir -p "$WORKTREE_PARENT"
16	git worktree add -b "$BRANCH" "$WORKTREE_PATH" >/dev/null 2>&1
17	
18	# Symlink shared config dirs — edits stay in sync across worktrees
19	log "  Symlinking config dirs..."
20	for d in .claude .cursor .instrumental .agent_os; do
21	  if [ -d "${REPO_PATH}/$d" ]; then
22	    ln -s "${REPO_PATH}/$d" "${WORKTREE_PATH}/$d"
23	    log "    linked $d"
24	  fi
25	done
26	
27	# Copy env files — these often need per-worktree tweaks
28	log "  Copying env files..."
29	for f in .env .env.local; do
30	  if [ -f "${REPO_PATH}/$f" ]; then
31	    cp "${REPO_PATH}/$f" "${WORKTREE_PATH}/$f"
32	    log "    copied $f"
33	  fi
34	done
35	
36	# Copy gitignored Jira config — tracked .jira files arrive via git checkout,
37	# but config.yml (and its backup) are per-clone and need to travel.
38	for f in .jira/config.yml .jira/config.yml.bkp; do
39	  if [ -f "${REPO_PATH}/$f" ]; then
40	    mkdir -p "${WORKTREE_PATH}/$(dirname "$f")"
41	    cp "${REPO_PATH}/$f" "${WORKTREE_PATH}/$f"
42	    log "    copied $f"
43	  fi
44	done
45	
46	log "Worktree ready."
47	
48	# The only thing on stdout — Claude parses this
49	echo "WORKTREE_PATH=$WORKTREE_PATH"
50	
```

## Assistant
The changes are clean and correct. The fix correctly addresses the root cause:

1. Removes the absolute-path symlink that broke CI runners
2. Adds the `.gitignore` guard to prevent re-addition
3. The idempotency condition in `create-worktree.sh` correctly handles all destination states (non-existent, valid symlink, broken symlink via `[ ! -L ]`), and `ln -sn` adds good defense-in-depth

Nothing to flag. Posting a top-level LGTM.

## Bash
```
gh pr comment 46 --body "Looks good to me. The fix is minimal and correct: the idempotency guard (\`[ ! -e ] && [ ! -L ]\`) covers all three destination states (absent, valid symlink, broken symlink), and \`ln -sn\` adds sound defense-in-depth. The \`.gitignore\` entry closes the re-add path cleanly."
```

## Result

```

content.thinking: The run was quite clean. Let me now read the agent prompt and workflow to understand what the agent is supposed to do and how it runs.

content.content:
1	You are the **PR Review agent** for this repository. The PR branch is already
2	checked out in the current working directory. The workflow has prepended `REPO`
3	and `PR NUMBER` lines above this prompt — use them.
4	
5	## Your job
6	
7	Review the changes in this PR and post review feedback as GitHub comments.
8	Nothing else.
9	
10	## What to look for
11	
12	Focus on these four buckets, in this order:
13	
14	1. **Correctness bugs** — logic errors, off-by-ones, wrong conditions,
15	   misuse of APIs, broken error handling, race conditions.
16	2. **Security issues** — injection risks, auth bypass, leaked secrets,
17	   unsafe deserialization, SSRF, missing input validation at trust
18	   boundaries.
19	3. **Performance** — only when the cost is clearly meaningful (N+1 queries,
20	   accidental quadratic loops, unbounded memory). Don't nitpick micro-perf.
21	4. **Code quality** — only when it materially hurts readability or
22	   maintainability. Don't enforce style preferences.
23	
24	Skim out of scope: formatting, naming bikeshedding, "have you considered…"
25	musings, hypothetical future requirements.
26	
27	## How to gather context
28	
29	- `gh pr view ${PR_NUMBER} --json title,body,state,additions,deletions,changedFiles,headRefName` — title, description, metadata. (Explicit `--json` fields avoid a `statusCheckRollup` permission error the integration token triggers with the default output.)
30	- `gh pr diff ${PR_NUMBER}` — the diff. This is the primary input. Don't use
31	  `git diff` — the checkout is shallow (`fetch-depth: 1`) so comparisons
32	  against main have no merge base and will fail.
33	- Read files in the working directory as needed to understand context around
34	  changed lines.
35	
36	### Verifying external facts
37	
38	Do not rely on training-data recall for anything externally verifiable —
39	library APIs, SDK versions, model names, CVE details, deprecation status.
40	Look it up before flagging it.
41	
42	- **Library / framework / SDK / API / CLI questions** — use Context7
43	  (`mcp__context7__resolve-library-id` then `mcp__context7__query-docs`).
44	  Prefer this over web search for docs.
45	- **Everything else** (model versions, CVEs, blog posts, changelogs not on
46	  Context7) — use `WebSearch`, then `WebFetch` for specific pages. When
47	  fetching package docs, prefer the GitHub source (e.g. the package README)
48	  over npmjs.com — npmjs.com commonly returns 403.
49	
50	If you can't verify a claim, either drop the comment or downgrade it to a
51	question rather than asserting a fact you're unsure about.
52	
53	## How to post feedback
54	
55	### Inline comments (primary)
56	
57	Specific issues go in inline comments via
58	`mcp__github_inline_comment__create_inline_comment` with `confirmed: true`.
59	One comment per distinct issue, anchored to the specific changed line. Each
60	comment must include:
61	
62	- A one-line description of the issue.
63	- **Severity:** one of `Critical` / `High` / `Medium` / `Low`.
64	- **Reasoning:** 1–3 sentences explaining *why* it's a problem and what to
65	  change. Be concrete; reference the code.
66	
67	Severity rubric:
68	- `Critical` — will break production, lose data, or expose a vulnerability.
69	- `High` — likely bug or security weakness; should block merge.
70	- `Medium` — real issue but contained; should be fixed before merge if cheap.
71	- `Low` — small correctness or clarity nit worth flagging.
72	
73	### High-level overview (optional)
74	
75	If the PR has a recurring pattern, an architectural concern, or anything
76	else that doesn't anchor to a single line, post one short top-level comment
77	via `gh pr comment ${PR_NUMBER} --body "..."`. Keep it brief — a few
78	sentences, not a recap of the inline comments. Skip this entirely if you
79	have nothing to add at that level. Escape any backticks in the `--body` string as `\`` to avoid shell interpretation.
80	
81	If the PR looks good and you have no inline issues to raise, post a brief
82	"Looks good to me" top-level comment via `gh pr comment` and stop.
83	
84	## When you're done
85	
86	Stop immediately after your last tool call. Do not list your findings, do not recap which comments you posted, do not confirm the comment count. The runner will exit.
87

file.content:
You are the **PR Review agent** for this repository. The PR branch is already
checked out in the current working directory. The workflow has prepended `REPO`
and `PR NUMBER` lines above this prompt — use them.

## Your job

Review the changes in this PR and post review feedback as GitHub comments.
Nothing else.

## What to look for

Focus on these four buckets, in this order:

1. **Correctness bugs** — logic errors, off-by-ones, wrong conditions,
   misuse of APIs, broken error handling, race conditions.
2. **Security issues** — injection risks, auth bypass, leaked secrets,
   unsafe deserialization, SSRF, missing input validation at trust
   boundaries.
3. **Performance** — only when the cost is clearly meaningful (N+1 queries,
   accidental quadratic loops, unbounded memory). Don't nitpick micro-perf.
4. **Code quality** — only when it materially hurts readability or
   maintainability. Don't enforce style preferences.

Skim out of scope: formatting, naming bikeshedding, "have you considered…"
musings, hypothetical future requirements.

## How to gather context

- `gh pr view ${PR_NUMBER} --json title,body,state,additions,deletions,changedFiles,headRefName` — title, description, metadata. (Explicit `--json` fields avoid a `statusCheckRollup` permission error the integration token triggers with the default output.)
- `gh pr diff ${PR_NUMBER}` — the diff. This is the primary input. Don't use
  `git diff` — the checkout is shallow (`fetch-depth: 1`) so comparisons
  against main have no merge base and will fail.
- Read files in the working directory as needed to understand context around
  changed lines.

### Verifying external facts

Do not rely on training-data recall for anything externally verifiable —
library APIs, SDK versions, model names, CVE details, deprecation status.
Look it up before flagging it.

- **Library / framework / SDK / API / CLI questions** — use Context7
  (`mcp__context7__resolve-library-id` then `mcp__context7__query-docs`).
  Prefer this over web search for docs.
- **Everything else** (model versions, CVEs, blog posts, changelogs not on
  Context7) — use `WebSearch`, then `WebFetch` for specific pages. When
  fetching package docs, prefer the GitHub source (e.g. the package README)
  over npmjs.com — npmjs.com commonly returns 403.

If you can't verify a claim, either drop the comment or downgrade it to a
question rather than asserting a fact you're unsure about.

## How to post feedback

### Inline comments (primary)

Specific issues go in inline comments via
`mcp__github_inline_comment__create_inline_comment` with `confirmed: true`.
One comment per distinct issue, anchored to the specific changed line. Each
comment must include:

- A one-line description of the issue.
- **Severity:** one of `Critical` / `High` / `Medium` / `Low`.
- **Reasoning:** 1–3 sentences explaining *why* it's a problem and what to
  change. Be concrete; reference the code.

Severity rubric:
- `Critical` — will break production, lose data, or expose a vulnerability.
- `High` — likely bug or security weakness; should block merge.
- `Medium` — real issue but contained; should be fixed before merge if cheap.
- `Low` — small correctness or clarity nit worth flagging.

### High-level overview (optional)

If the PR has a recurring pattern, an architectural concern, or anything
else that doesn't anchor to a single line, post one short top-level comment
via `gh pr comment ${PR_NUMBER} --body "..."`. Keep it brief — a few
sentences, not a recap of the inline comments. Skip this entirely if you
have nothing to add at that level. Escape any backticks in the `--body` string as `\`` to avoid shell interpretation.

If the PR looks good and you have no inline issues to raise, post a brief
"Looks good to me" top-level comment via `gh pr comment` and stop.

## When you're done

Stop immediately after your last tool call. Do not list your findings, do not recap which comments you posted, do not confirm the comment count. The runner will exit.

content.content:
1	name: PR Review agent
2	
3	on:
4	  pull_request:
5	    types: [opened, ready_for_review, reopened]
6	    paths-ignore:
7	      - '.github/agent-logs/**'
8	      - 'agents/**/prompt.md'
9	  workflow_dispatch:
10	
11	jobs:
12	  review:
13	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
14	    permissions:
15	      contents: write
16	      pull-requests: write
17	      id-token: write
18	      actions: write
19	      checks: read
20	      statuses: read
21	    steps:
22	      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
23	        with:
24	          fetch-depth: 1
25	
26	      - id: prompt
27	        run: |
28	          {
29	            echo 'PROMPT<<PROMPT_EOF'
30	            echo "REPO: ${{ github.repository }}"
31	            echo "PR NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}"
32	            echo
33	            cat agents/pr-review/prompt.md
34	            echo PROMPT_EOF
35	          } >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
36	
37	      - id: claude
38	        uses: anthropics/claude-code-action@v1
39	        with:
40	          claude_code_oauth_token: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
41	          track_progress: false
42	          prompt: ${{ steps.prompt.outputs.PROMPT }}
43	          settings: |
44	            { "env": { "CONTEXT7_API_KEY": "${{ secrets.CONTEXT7_API_KEY }}" } }
45	          claude_args: |

48	      - name: Commit execution log to PR branch
49	        if: always() && steps.claude.outputs.execution_file != ''
50	        env:
51	          PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
52	          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
53	          LOG_PATH: ${{ steps.claude.outputs.execution_file }}
54	          BRANCH: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.ref }}
55	        run: |
56	          set -e
57	          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
58	          git config user.email "41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
59	          git fetch --depth=1 origin "$BRANCH"
60	          git checkout -B "$BRANCH" "origin/$BRANCH"
61	          DEST=".github/agent-logs/pr-review/pr-${PR_NUMBER}/${RUN_ID}.json"
62	          mkdir -p "$(dirname "$DEST")"
63	          cp "$LOG_PATH" "$DEST"
64	          git add "$DEST"
65	          git commit -m "agent log: PR #${PR_NUMBER} run ${RUN_ID}"
66	          git push origin "$BRANCH"
67	
68	      - name: Trigger agent improver
69	        if: always() && steps.claude.outputs.execution_file != ''
70	        env:
71	          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
72	          PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
73	          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
74	          LOG_REF: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.ref }}
75	        run: |
76	          gh workflow run agent-improver.yml \
77	            -f agent_name=pr-review \
78	            -f log_path=".github/agent-logs/pr-review/pr-${PR_NUMBER}/${RUN_ID}.json" \

file.content:
name: PR Review agent

on:
  pull_request:
    types: [opened, ready_for_review, reopened]
    paths-ignore:
      - '.github/agent-logs/**'
      - 'agents/**/prompt.md'
  workflow_dispatch:

jobs:
  review:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: write
      pull-requests: write
      id-token: write
      actions: write
      checks: read
      statuses: read
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
        with:
          fetch-depth: 1

      - id: prompt
        run: |
          {
            echo 'PROMPT<<PROMPT_EOF'
            echo "REPO: ${{ github.repository }}"
            echo "PR NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}"
            echo
            cat agents/pr-review/prompt.md
            echo PROMPT_EOF
          } >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - id: claude
        uses: anthropics/claude-code-action@v1
        with:
          claude_code_oauth_token: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
          track_progress: false
          prompt: ${{ steps.prompt.outputs.PROMPT }}
          settings: |
            { "env": { "CONTEXT7_API_KEY": "${{ secrets.CONTEXT7_API_KEY }}" } }
          claude_args: |
            --allowedTools "mcp__github_inline_comment__create_inline_comment,mcp__context7__resolve-library-id,mcp__context7__query-docs,WebSearch,WebFetch,Bash(gh pr comment:*),Bash(gh pr diff:*),Bash(gh pr view:*),Read,Grep,Glob"

      - name: Commit execution log to PR branch
        if: always() && steps.claude.outputs.execution_file != ''
        env:
          PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
          LOG_PATH: ${{ steps.claude.outputs.execution_file }}
          BRANCH: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.ref }}
        run: |
          set -e
          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
          git config user.email "41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
          git fetch --depth=1 origin "$BRANCH"
          git checkout -B "$BRANCH" "origin/$BRANCH"
          DEST=".github/agent-logs/pr-review/pr-${PR_NUMBER}/${RUN_ID}.json"
          mkdir -p "$(dirname "$DEST")"
          cp "$LOG_PATH" "$DEST"
          git add "$DEST"
          git commit -m "agent log: PR #${PR_NUMBER} run ${RUN_ID}"
          git push origin "$BRANCH"

      - name: Trigger agent improver
        if: always() && steps.claude.outputs.execution_file != ''
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
          LOG_REF: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.ref }}
        run: |
          gh workflow run agent-improver.yml \
            -f agent_name=pr-review \
            -f log_path=".github/agent-logs/pr-review/pr-${PR_NUMBER}/${RUN_ID}.json" \
            -f log_ref="${LOG_REF}"

content.thinking:
Let me analyze this run:

1. The run fetched PR details and diff
2. Reviewed the changes (removing the self-referential `.claude/.claude` symlink)
3. Posted a top-level "LGTM" comment

The run was very clean and efficient:
- Only 3 tool calls (gh pr view, gh pr diff, gh pr comment) + one Read to verify context
- The agent correctly identified no issues with the PR
- Posted a concise LGTM comment
- Stopped immediately after posting

Looking for any potential improvements:
- The agent did read `create-worktree.sh` even though it wasn't in the diff. 

The file read was meant to provide context, but the diff already showed the complete change, so it was unnecessary work. I also notice the agent included some explanatory text before posting the comment, which goes against the instruction to stop immediately after the final tool call without recapping findings. The file read gave useful context to verify the diff was accurate against the actual script on the PR branch. Overall this was a clean, efficient run—the agent gathered metadata, reviewed the diff, checked the implementation, and posted approval without unnecessary commentary.
